management_notes:
President and CEO Tara Christie is the second-largest shareholder with approximately 3.4% of shares outstanding and brings 25+ years of Yukon exploration and mine development experience. She led AurMac from grassroots discovery to a 7.7 Moz resource — the project that now defines the company's primary value. VP Exploration Duncan MacKay (appointed 2025) serves as the NI 43-101 Qualified Person for AurMac. CFO David Rutt adds capital markets depth.

Franco-Nevada's C$52.2M NSR royalty purchase (January 2026) is the single most credible external endorsement available in the junior mining market — a major royalty company does not deploy $52M on a pre-PEA project without conviction in the asset quality, management team, and district potential. The 2025 capital raise of C$45.9M without issuing any warrants across multiple closings is also notable: it demonstrates institutional demand and management's conviction that warrant-free financing was achievable. Christie's share price is up 350%+ in 2025, reflecting both gold price leverage and management execution.

Insider ownership of approximately 6.4% combined (Christie ~3.4%, David Reid ~3.0%) is adequate but not exceptional for a company of this stage. The overall alignment picture is positive given Franco-Nevada's royalty as a de facto institutional imprimatur.
geology_notes:
The AurMac Project (720 km², Tombstone Gold Belt, Yukon) hosts a NI 43-101 compliant resource (effective June 28, 2025): 2.274 Moz indicated at 0.63 g/t Au and 5.453 Moz inferred at 0.60 g/t Au, for a combined 7.727 Moz total at AurMac. The Hyland Gold Project adds 337,000 oz Au indicated at approximately 2.5 g/t Au plus 2.63 Moz Ag indicated, bringing the combined portfolio resource above 7.7 Moz AuEq. The NI 43-101 Technical Reports for both projects were filed in August and October 2025, respectively.

The deposit style at AurMac is a Reduced Intrusion-Related Gold System (RIRGS) — bulk-tonnage, low-grade gold suitable for large-scale open-pit or bulk underground mining. At 0.60-0.63 g/t Au, the grade is low by high-grade standards but large enough by tonnage (393 Mt total) to support a world-scale mine at appropriate strip ratios. A PEA (scoping study) targeting Q4 2026 will be the first economics-level assessment of the deposit and the key inflection point for institutional interest. Metallurgical work and geotechnical studies are underway as PEA preparation.

A high-grade silver vein discovery at AurMac adds a separate geological dimension: drill results including 3,408 g/t Ag over 1.4 m and 880 g/t Ag over 1.5 m suggest a Keno-style silver vein system overlying the bulk RIRGS gold system. This high-grade component could add a higher-grade starter-pit or underground component to the PEA and meaningfully improve early-year economics.
capital_notes:
Banyan Gold has approximately 413 million basic shares outstanding (443.5 million fully diluted) and approximately C$42M in cash as of October 2025, with the company explicitly funded through 2027. The 2025 capital raises totalling C$45.9M were conducted entirely without warrants — a discipline rarely seen in TSXV financing markets that limits warrant overhang and demonstrates genuine institutional demand at market prices. Options account for the 30.2M-share dilution between basic and fully diluted counts.

Franco-Nevada's C$52.2M NSR royalty purchase provided the largest non-dilutive capital injection in the company's history and substantially extended the cash runway without issuing a single new equity share. The 40,000-metre 2026 drill program (commenced February 2026, four active rigs) is explicitly funded within the existing treasury through 2027. The C$574M market cap is large for a pre-PEA company but reflects the scale of the 7.7 Moz resource and the franco-Nevada endorsement.

The capital structure is clean by TSXV standards. No debt, no excessive warrant overhang, and a fully funded multi-year exploration plan. The main risk is that the PEA economics (expected Q4 2026) may reveal a capex requirement (typical for a Yukon bulk-tonnage gold project: C$500M-1B+) that will necessitate a significant equity raise before any construction decision.
catalyst_notes:
The active 40,000-metre diamond drill program (commenced February 2026, four rigs) at AurMac is the primary near-term news driver, with results expected on a rolling basis through summer and fall 2026. The program is approximately 80% focused on Airstrip and Powerline targets — the highest-grade zones within the AurMac system — which have returned results including 3.95 g/t Au over 38.1 m at Airstrip and 27.9 g/t Au over 1.5 m at Powerline.

The Q4 2026 PEA (scoping study) for AurMac is the most significant catalyst in the 12-month horizon. The PEA will establish economics for the first time, enabling institutional investors and potential acquirers to model net present values and construction timelines. Positive PEA economics (reasonable capex vs. NPV at current gold prices) could catalyse a significant re-rating, particularly given Franco-Nevada's endorsement.

The silver vein system discovery adds a near-term exploration catalyst independent of the bulk-gold resource — if confirmed with additional drilling, it could add a high-grade mining component that improves early-year cash flows in the PEA. Additional Hyland drill results are also possible in 2026.
acquisition_notes:
Banyan's enterprise value of approximately C$532M implies approximately US$52/oz on the combined 7.727 Moz total AurMac resource — a discount to Yukon peer transactions at US$60-300/oz. The wide range of peer multiples reflects the spectrum from early-resource (lower) to feasibility-stage (higher), and BYN's pre-PEA status appropriately places it at the lower end of the range.

Franco-Nevada's C$52.2M NSR royalty purchase is the clearest M&A-adjacent signal available to the market — royalty companies of Franco's calibre do not invest at this scale in pre-PEA assets without high conviction in the project's eventual production. This effectively provides a floor valuation for the deposit. However, the NSR itself limits future equity upside, as approximately C$52M in royalty payments are pre-committed to Franco-Nevada from future production.

The PEA in Q4 2026 will be the decisive moment for acquirer interest. A Yukon bulk-tonnage gold project of 7.7 Moz scale at current gold prices could attract attention from senior and mid-tier producers seeking reserve replacement, particularly given the Tombstone Gold Belt's tier-1 jurisdiction and established operating precedents (Alexco, Victoria Gold).
analyst_summary:
WATCH (19/25). Banyan Gold has built one of the largest pre-PEA gold resources in the Yukon — 7.727 Moz at AurMac — with clean capital structure (no warrants from recent financings), C$42M in cash, and a fully funded 40,000-metre 2026 drill program. Franco-Nevada's C$52.2M NSR royalty purchase is the highest-quality third-party endorsement available to junior mining investors and provides a credible floor valuation for the asset. The silver vein discovery at AurMac adds a high-grade optionality component to an otherwise bulk-tonnage story.

The primary limitation is the pre-PEA status: until the Q4 2026 scoping study establishes economics, the stock is priced on resource scale and endorsement signals rather than modelled cash flows. The low gold grade (0.60-0.63 g/t Au) at AurMac means unit economics will be highly sensitive to the assumed gold price, strip ratio, and throughput — and a large-scale Yukon gold mine will require C$500M-1B+ in construction financing, well beyond the current treasury. NSR royalty terms limit equity upside relative to a royalty-free project.

The key catalyst to watch is the Q4 2026 PEA for AurMac. Positive economics at a reasonable capex intensity (less than US$200M per 100,000 oz of annual production) could catalyse a re-rating to BUY and likely attract multiple acquisition inquiries. Interim 2026 drill results from the high-grade Airstrip and Powerline zones will shape expectations heading into the PEA release.
